Weather Today [23 May, 2026]: Check IMD Rain Predictions, Heat Alert, Snow & Thunderstorms Across Delhi NCR, Mumbai, Chennai, Karnataka, Rajasthan, Jammu, Hyderabad & More — Full City-Wise Forecast - The Sunday Guardian,https://news.google.?oc=5,The Sunday Guardian,2026-05-23T06:22:33+05:30,en,Karnataka,Kodagu,"""heatwave"" Karnataka","Weather Today, 23 May 2026: India’s weather map on May 23 presents two sharply different realities. Northern and central states are battling punishing heatwave conditions, while southern and northeastern regions are witnessing thunderstorms and heavy rainfall activity. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued multiple alerts as temperatures in parts of Uttar Pradesh, Delhi, Rajasthan and Madhya Pradesh continue to climb above 45°C while at the same time, Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu are preparing for intense pre-monsoon showers.

Meteorologists say this unusual split highlights the growing intensity of seasonal weather extremes across the country while heat retention in urban regions, delayed moisture movement and changing wind circulation patterns are accelerating temperature spikes earlier than expected.
Will it Rain or Snow in the Hills?

Higher reaches of Jammu and Kashmir and Himachal Pradesh may witness light snowfall in isolated areas above 3,000 metres, especially near Gulmarg and Sonamarg. Rainfall activity is also expected in parts of Uttarakhand during evening hours while srinagar is likely to remain cloudy with cool winds.
Why Is The Heat Building Up So Fast Across India?
Weather experts attribute the rapid rise in temperatures to three major factors:
- Dry continental winds moving from Rajasthan into the Indo-Gangetic plains
- Weak cloud formation across north India
- Urban heat island effect in densely populated cities

More than 30 cities in Uttar Pradesh recorded temperatures above 43°C in the last 24 hours while East Uttar Pradesh touched 47.6°C, among the highest temperatures recorded globally this week. India has also experienced below-normal western disturbances this month, allowing direct solar heating to intensify.
Where are the Heat Wave Alerts?
Red alerts have been issued in several districts of eastern Uttar Pradesh.
Is Monsoon Arrived?

The southwest monsoon has advanced over parts of the southern Arabian Sea, Maldives and sections of the Bay of Bengal however, it has not officially reached mainland Kerala yet. Pre-monsoon rainfall has intensified across Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu, signalling gradual monsoon progression.

How Will Rising Temperatures Impact?

Rising temperatures are already affecting electricity demand, groundwater usage and public health while hospitals in north India are reporting increased cases of dehydration, heat exhaustion and respiratory distress. Agricultural experts warn that prolonged heat may damage wheat storage, vegetable crops and dairy productivity while urban centres are also witnessing rising air pollution due to stagnant atmospheric conditions.

Delhi’s Air Quality Today – May 23, 2026

Delhi’s AQI remained in the “Poor” category due to dust accumulation and stagnant winds. Several monitoring stations reported AQI levels between 240 and 290 while health experts advise children, elderly residents and respiratory patients to avoid prolonged outdoor exposure.
